dict.string: Kaka uses the camera to take photos for local agent editing and to scan pairing QR codes.
Agent Pocket connects to your local agent runtime on your local network.
Kaka may use your approximate location only when you include a task-scoped Context Snapshot, so your local runtime can understand the current situation without storing precise coordinates by default.
Kaka records audio only when you press the voice button so it can turn your request into an editable transcript before sending text to your local agent runtime.
Kaka may read motion state only when you include a task-scoped Context Snapshot, so your local runtime can adapt the response without background tracking.
Agent Pocket saves edited photos only when you tap Save.
Agent Pocket lets you choose photos to edit through your local agent runtime.
Kaka may check calendar availability only when you include a task-scoped Context Snapshot; event titles, attendees, notes, and event bodies are not sent by default.
Kaka uses speech recognition only after you press the voice button to turn that recording into an editable transcript.
